model:
# Tableau → Power BI Semantic Model Migration Constitution

> **UNIVERSAL** — This constitution is shared across ALL workbook migrations in this
> workspace and is NEVER overwritten per workbook. It is the single authority for how any
> Tableau workbook (.twb) is converted into a Power BI Project (.pbip) semantic model.

## Core Principles

### I. Star Schema First
- Design a star schema: one (or more) fact tables surrounded by conformed dimension tables.
- Fact table grain = the lowest level of detail of a single source row (one row = one event/record).
- Dimension candidates are categorical/descriptive columns (text, low-cardinality, lookup attributes).
- Single-source flat extracts MAY remain a single flat table (`single-flat` strategy) when no
  conformed dimensions provide analytic value; document the choice.
- Multi-value delimited fields (comma-separated lists such as `cast`, `listed_in`, `country`)
  MAY be (a) kept flat for fidelity, (b) split + bridged via a many-to-many bridge table with
  natural keys, or (c) normalized into a dimension. The star-schema agent decides and documents.
- Always create a `DimDate` (Calendar) table when any date field exists; mark it as a date table.

### II. Naming Conventions
- Tables: business-friendly PascalCase or natural names (e.g. `netflix_titles`, `DimDate`).
- Columns: keep source names where meaningful; Title Case for display where it improves clarity.
- Measures: descriptive Title Case (e.g. `Title Count`, `CY Sales`); never reuse a column name.
- No reserved characters that require escaping unless inherited from source; quote only when needed.

### III. DAX Standards
- Prefer **measures** over calculated columns (DAX best practice) unless a physical column is required
  for a relationship, slicer, sort-by, or row-context grouping.
- Use `DIVIDE()` for safe division; `COUNTROWS`, `DISTINCTCOUNT` for counts; `VAR`/`RETURN` for clarity.
- Use `IF()` for simple branches, `SWITCH(TRUE(), …)` for multi-branch logic.
- Tableau table calculations map to: running/window → `RANKX` / `OFFSET` / `WINDOW` / `MAXX`;
  LOD expressions → `CALCULATE` with `REMOVEFILTERS` / `VALUES` / `ALLEXCEPT`.
- Organize measures into display folders (e.g. `KPIs`, `Counts`).
- **CALCULATE boolean filters MUST NOT reference a measure directly** — capture into a VAR first.
- Top-N at the visual layer is implemented via a DAX rank measure + an Advanced/comparison
  filter (`rank <= N`); never via a `filterConfig` at the PBIR visual root.

### IV. Relationships
- Single-source → natural keys. Multi-source / joined → keep join keys or introduce surrogate keys.
- One-to-many from dimension (one side) to fact (many side); single cross-filter by default.
- Use bidirectional cross-filter only when a measure must traverse a bridge/security table.
- Mark inactive relationships explicitly; activate via `USERELATIONSHIP` in measures when needed.

### V. M Query (Power Query) Safety
- CSV: `Csv.Document(File.Contents(<abs path>), [Delimiter=…, Encoding=65001, QuoteStyle=QuoteStyle.Csv])`
  then `Table.PromoteHeaders(…, [PromoteAllScalars=true])`.
- SQL Server: `Sql.Database(server, db)`; PostgreSQL: `PostgreSQL.Database(…)`; Excel: `Excel.Workbook(File.Contents(…))`.
- Always resolve data files to ABSOLUTE paths under the workspace `Data/{subfolder}/` directory.
- Apply explicit `Table.TransformColumnTypes` for numeric/date columns; specify culture where decimal separators differ.
- M expression (query) names MUST NOT collide with table names.

### VI. PBIP Structure
- Emit `{Model}.pbip`, `{Model}.SemanticModel/`, and `{Model}.Report/` under `Output/{WorkbookName}/`.
- `definition.pbism` version MUST be `4.2`; `definition.pbir` version MUST be `4.0`; `.pbip` MUST carry `$schema` and MUST NOT include a `dataset` artifact.
- Semantic model uses the TMDL `definition/` folder (database.tmdl, model.tmdl, relationships.tmdl, tables/*.tmdl).
- Every file is UTF-8 **without BOM**.
- `.platform` files carry correct `metadata.type`, `metadata.displayName`, and a GUID `config.logicalId`.

### VII. Parameter Mapping
- Integer/numeric range parameter → What-If parameter (`GENERATESERIES` disconnected table).
- String/list parameter → field parameter or a disconnected slicer table.
- Date parameter → `DimDate` slicer or a disconnected date table; resolve in the DAX/decisions stage.

## Validation Checklist (Quality Gate)
- TMDL passes `tmdl-validate` (indentation, property order, quoting, nesting, referential integrity).
- `validate_pbip.py` returns 0 errors (warnings/SCHEMA_DEGRADED are acceptable).
- All `ref table` entries have matching `tables/{Name}.tmdl` files.
- All relationships resolve to existing tables/columns.
- Entry-point file versions correct (`4.2` pbism, `4.0` pbir); all JSON has `$schema`.
- PBIP opens in Power BI Desktop without errors; all tables load; measures calculate.

## Governance
- This constitution supersedes ad-hoc decisions; deviations must be documented in the workbook spec.
- It is UNIVERSAL and read-only per migration — never regenerate or overwrite it for a single workbook.
- Edge cases not covered here are recorded as clarifications in the per-workbook `spec.md`.

**Version**: 1.0.0 | **Ratified**: 2026-06-16 | **Last Amended**: 2026-06-16
report:
# Report Constitution: Visual Layer Standards

> **UNIVERSAL** — Shared across ALL workbook migrations; NEVER overwritten per workbook.

## Canvas & Layout

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Canvas Size | 1280 × 720 px (16:9) |
| Edge Padding (Top) | 25px |
| Edge Padding (Left/Right) | 25px |
| Inter-Visual Gap | **20px minimum** — visuals MUST NOT overlap |
| Max Visuals Per Page | 8–10 (avoid clutter) |
| Alignment | Grid-aligned, consistent row heights |

> **CRITICAL GAP RULE**: When calculating visual positions, ensure `visual_x + visual_width + 20px ≤ next_visual_x` (horizontal) and `visual_y + visual_height + 20px ≤ next_visual_y` (vertical). Visuals MUST NEVER overlap. Subtract gap from calculated width/height, not add to position.

## Typography

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Font Family | Aptos |
| Data Font Size | 10pt |
| Visual Title Size | 12pt bold |
| Page Title Size | 14pt bold |
| Axis Labels | 10pt |
| Data Labels | 10pt |
| Slicer Text | 10pt |
| Tooltip Text | 10pt |

## Theme & Colors

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Background | Professional — White (#FFFFFF) canvas |
| Visual Background | Light gray (#F5F5F5) or White |
| Border | **ENABLED** — 1px solid #E0E0E0 on ALL visuals |
| Border Radius | 4px (subtle rounded corners) |
| Shadow | None (clean professional look) |
| Primary Color | #2C3E50 (dark blue-gray) |
| Accent Colors | Professional palette: #3498DB, #2ECC71, #E74C3C, #F39C12, #9B59B6, #1ABC9C |
| Gridlines | Light (#EBEBEB), minimal |
| Title Color | #2C3E50 |
| Data Color | #333333 |

## Table/Matrix Visual Rules

| Data Type | Alignment | Format |
|---|---|---|
| Numbers | Left-aligned | Preserve source format (commas, decimals) |
| Text | Right-aligned | As-is |
| Dates | Left-aligned | Preserve source format (dd/MM/yyyy or as source) |
| Currency | Left-aligned | Source currency symbol + 2 decimal |
| Percentage | Left-aligned | Source format with % |

## Data Format Preservation

- **Principle**: Data format must remain the same as the source Tableau workbook
- Number of decimal places: Same as source
- Currency symbol: Same as source
- Date format pattern: Same as source
- Thousands separator: Same as source
- Do NOT auto-convert formats unless Tableau format is incompatible with Power BI

## Visual Behavior

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Responsive | Enabled |
| Data Labels | Show where space permits (≤6 data points) |
| Legend | Show only when multiple series |
| Axis Titles | Show when not self-explanatory |
| Tooltips | Enable with relevant measures |
| Sort | Preserve Tableau sort order |

## Slicer Standards

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Style | Dropdown (for >5 items), List (for ≤5) |
| Position | Top row or right panel |
| Multi-select | Enabled by default |
| "Select All" | Show for multi-select slicers |
| Font | Aptos 10pt |
| **Title** | **DISABLED (`show: false`)** — slicers use header only |
| **Header** | **ENABLED** — shows the field name as the slicer label |

> **CRITICAL**: Slicers must NEVER have both title and header active. Use ONLY the slicer header (built-in field label). Set `visualContainerObjects.title.show = false` for all slicers.

## Page Layout Strategy

1. **Title bar** (top 50px): Page title + key slicers
2. **KPI row** (next 100px): Card visuals for key metrics
3. **Main area** (remaining): Charts and tables in grid
4. **If dashboard has >8 visuals**: Split into multiple pages with logical grouping

## Naming Conventions

| Item | Convention |
|---|---|
| Page names | Same as Tableau dashboard name (Title Case) |
| Visual titles | Same as Tableau worksheet name (descriptive) |
| Bookmark names | Action + Context (e.g., "Show Details") |
